performance.issue: Dimensions.get('window') called at module scope. Static value calculated once at import time; does not update on rotation/resize. Consider useWindowDimensions in consuming components.
Dimensions.get('window') called at module scope for screenWidth. Static layout values baked in at import; won't adapt to orientation changes.
DAY_WIDTH computed from Dimensions.get('window') at module scope. Computed once, stale on rotation. A getDayWidth helper exists on line 4 but DAY_WIDTH is still exported and likely consumed.
SCREEN_WIDTH from Dimensions.get('window') at module scope. CARD_WIDTH derived from it statically. getCardWidth helper exists on line 5 but SCREEN_WIDTH/CARD_WIDTH constants are still exported.
SCREEN_WIDTH from Dimensions.get('window') at module scope. METER_WIDTH derived statically; won't adapt to rotation.
Device.width and Device.height from Dimensions.get('window') at module scope. These are static and never update. Any component consuming Device.width/height will be stale on rotation.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onPress(); }. Creates new function reference every render; causes child re-render. Wrap in useCallback or pass onPress directly.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); (onPress || onRemove)?.(); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onDismiss(); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop with multi-line body including analytics tracking. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: (e) => { buttonTap(); props.onPress?.(e); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onDismiss(); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop with analytics tracking body. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onAction(); }. Repeated at lines 86 and 150. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop at lines 102, 115, 186. Multiple instances of () => { buttonTap(); onAction(); } creating new references each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop at lines 165 and 178. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => navigation.goBack(). New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => onAchievementPress(currentToast.id). New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onActionPress(message); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); setExpanded(!expanded); }. New reference each render. Also at line 128.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop with analytics tracking body. New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => setError(null). New reference each render.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onPress?.(); }. New reference each render.
Inline arrow functions in onPress props for GambleOption components at lines 133, 140, 147. New references each render: () => onStartGamble('CONSERVATIVE') etc.
Inline arrow function in onPress prop: () => { buttonTap(); onPress(); }. New reference each render.

state_violations.issue: useQuery used for achievements data fetching — appropriate for server state. No violation.

Three separate useQuery calls (state, profile, messages) could be consolidated into a single query or useQueries for reduced waterfall. Each query independently fetches, causing sequential network requests.
useUpdateChallengeProgress mutation has no onError handler. Errors are silently swallowed — no Sentry capture, no user-facing toast on failure.
useRerollChallenge mutation has no onError handler. Reroll failures are silently lost.
useAddPlanItem mutation has no onError handler. Plan item creation failures silently swallowed.
useUpdatePlanItemStatus mutation has no onError handler.
useDeletePlanItem mutation has no onError handler.
useAddProject mutation has no onError handler.
useAddStudyPlan mutation has no onError handler.
useCaptureMutation has no onError handler. Capture submission failures silently lost.
useSyncOnboardingProgress mutation has no onError handler. Onboarding sync failures silently swallowed.
submitMutation has no onError handler on the mutation itself. Error handling is done in the wrapping submit() callback, but the raw mutation error path is unhandled.
useCreateRecommendation mutation has no onError handler. Recommendation creation failures not tracked in Sentry.
useUpdateRecommendationStatus mutation has no onError handler.
useCreateFocusContract mutation has no onError handler.
useReflectOnFocusContract mutation has no onError handler.

error_handling.issue: setTimeout used for auto-dismiss without try/catch around hide(). If hide() throws, the error is unhandled.
setTimeout used for scrollToEnd without error handling. If flashListRef.current is null between timeout set and fire, could throw.
setTimeout inside useEffect for animation without try/catch. If animation values are stale, withRepeat could throw.
setTimeout for onPress callback without error handling. If onPress throws, error is unhandled.
await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, delay)) in retry loop without error handling around the delay itself.

setInterval inside useEffect — error handling exists inside the callback (try/catch), but the setInterval itself is not wrapped. If setInterval throws (unlikely but possible with invalid interval), the error would be unhandled.

memory_leaks.issue: Nested setTimeout inside useEffect not cleaned up. Line 96 sets outer timeout (cleaned at line 102), but the inner setTimeout at line 98 (500ms delay for onComplete) is fire-and-forget. If the component unmounts after the outer timeout fires but before the inner one, onComplete runs on an unmounted component.

SessionCompletionOfflineSyncService.startAutoSync() creates a setInterval (line 139) and a network subscription (line 136) but the class has no stop() or destroy() method. The service is a singleton (line 162), so the interval runs for the entire app lifecycle and is never cleaned up.

setTimeout used for TTL-based channel key cleanup: setTimeout(() => channelKeys.delete(key), options.ttlMs). No clearTimeout — relies on GC. If the channel is removed before TTL, the stale timeout still fires and tries to delete a potentially-reused key.

setInterval assigned to intervalRef.current inside AppState change handler. The cleanup at line 144-146 removes the AppState subscription but does NOT clear the intervalRef. If the component unmounts while the interval is running (set on line 125), the interval leaks.

AppState.addEventListener subscription cleaned at line 120. However, the sessionQuery.backgroundSession().catch() call at line 126 is fire-and-forget on unmount — if the user navigates away rapidly, the promise may resolve after component is unmounted.

setInterval for pulse animation (2s interval) properly cleaned up on line 82. However, the interval is recreated on every [isPulsing, pulseStart, isReducedMotion, pulseScale] dependency change without clearing the previous one first — the cleanup function runs before the new effect, so this is actually fine. No leak.

render_issues.issue: FlashList with estimatedItemSize={80}. Chat messages vary significantly in height (short replies vs long coaching messages). A more accurate estimate would reduce layout shift.
FlashList with estimatedItemSize={80} but the list mixes header items and notification items of varying heights. The uniform estimate may cause significant layout recalculation.

FlashList height calculated as Math.max(360, history.length * 86) — this forces the entire list to be laid out at once, defeating FlashList's virtualization. scrollEnabled={false} further prevents any scrolling benefit.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} — this prevents virtualization from working. The entire list renders at once. Consider using a simple ScrollView with a map if scrolling is disabled.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} — same issue as ChallengeHub. Virtualization disabled; full render of all items.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} — virtualization disabled. Full render.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} — virtualization disabled. Full render.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} — virtualization disabled. Full render.
FlashList with scrollEnabled={false} and horizontal — virtualization disabled for a horizontal list of themes.
Global Device.width/height from Dimensions.get('window') at module scope. Any component importing these values gets a stale snapshot that never updates on rotation or resize.
useReducedMotion hook is well-implemented and widely used across 20+ components (animations, modals, confetti, coach avatar, buttons, etc.). No issues found — this is a positive pattern.
